title: quercus faginea subvar. microphylla
intro: Quercus faginea subvar. microphylla, also known as the dwarf Portuguese oak or small-leaved Portuguese oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree that belongs to the Quercus genus and the Fagaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Quercus faginea subvar. microphylla include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions and decoctions, which can be used to make teas and tinctures that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
